info.name: CollabCanvas API
info.description:
Complete and verified REST API collection for CollabCanvas. All routes are confirmed against auth.js, rooms.js, notifications.js, and canvasController.js.

Base URL: http://localhost:5000

Test flow:
1. Login → jwt_token saved automatically
2. Create Room OR Join Room → roomId saved automatically
3. All other endpoints just work

NOTE: Drawing (strokes, shapes, text, layers, undo/redo, cursor, chat) is handled exclusively via Socket.IO WebSocket, NOT REST endpoints.

variable.description: Auto-populated by Login User test script. Copy manually if needed.
Auto-populated by Create Room or Join Room test scripts. MongoDB _id of the room.
Short alphanumeric room code (e.g. AB12CD34). Used in Join Room body. Get from Create Room response.
Canvas version _id. Auto-populated by Save Canvas test script.
Notification _id. Set from Get Notifications response.
MongoDB user _id of a room participant. Used in Manage Participants.

request.description: Registers a new user. Body field is 'fullName' (not 'displayName') — the controller maps it to displayName internally. Sends a verification email.
Logs in and auto-saves JWT to {{jwt_token}}. If 2FA is enabled, response returns requiresTwoFactor:true — proceed to Verify 2FA instead.
Authenticates via Google OAuth. Body field: 'credential' (the Google ID token string from the Google Sign-In button). Route: POST /api/auth/google-login
Verifies a user's email address using the token sent to their inbox after registration.
Completes login for users with 2FA enabled. Called after Login returns requiresTwoFactor:true.
Enables or disables Two-Factor Authentication for the authenticated user.
Sends a password reset email to the given address with a reset token link.
Resets password using the token from the reset email. Body field is 'password' (not 'newPassword').
Changes the authenticated user's password. Requires current password for verification.
Permanently deletes the authenticated user's account. Route: DELETE /api/auth/delete-account
Checks if a username is available (not taken). Public endpoint, no auth needed. Replace 'johndoe' with the username to check. Route: GET /api/auth/check-username/:username
Searches users by username or displayName. Query param 'q' must be at least 2 characters. Returns up to 20 results excluding the requesting user. Route: GET /api/auth/search?q=<query>
Returns the authenticated user's full profile. Route: GET /api/auth/profile (all profile endpoints live under /api/auth/, not /api/profile/)

Updates profile fields. All fields optional. 'avatar' accepts a URL or base64 string. 'notificationSettings' keys: email, push, reminders, marketing, securityAlerts, soundEnabled, desktopNotifications, notificationFrequency. Route: PUT /api/auth/update-profile

Creates a new collaborative drawing room. Use 'isPublic' (boolean) for visibility. 'maxParticipants' minimum is 2. The test script auto-saves room._id into {{roomId}}. Check the console for the roomCode to share with others. Route: POST /api/rooms/create

Joins a room using its short alphanumeric room code (e.g. 'AB12CD34'). Set {{roomCode}} in collection variables or replace inline. Leave 'password' blank for public rooms. The test script auto-saves room._id into {{roomId}}.

Validates if a room exists and checks the user's access (banned, member, etc.). Accepts both MongoDB _id and roomCode as the :id parameter. Returns requiresPassword, isAlreadyMember flags. Route: GET /api/rooms/:id/validate
Returns paginated public rooms. Public endpoint, no auth needed. Query params: page, limit, sort (newest/popular/name), search (optional room name filter).
Returns all rooms the authenticated user has created or joined (excluding banned rooms). Route: GET /api/rooms/my-rooms
Returns the participant list for a room. Route: GET /api/rooms/:id/participants
Invites users to the room. Body: { userIds: string[] } — array of MongoDB user _id values. Requires owner or moderator role. Use endpoint 12 (Search Users) to find user IDs.

Admin actions on a participant. 'action' options: kick, ban, promote (to moderator), demote (to participant). Only owners/moderators can perform actions. Route: POST /api/rooms/:id/participants/:userId
Removes the authenticated user from the room. Owners cannot leave — they must delete the room instead.

Updates room settings. Owner only. Use 'isPublic' (boolean) — backend translates it to the 'visibility' field. Protected fields (_id, owner, roomCode, participants, isActive, drawingData) cannot be updated.
Soft-deletes a room (sets isActive:false). Owner only.
Exports the current canvas drawing. 'format' options: png (binary image), svg (XML text), json (raw drawing data). Set Postman to save response as a file for binary formats.

Saves the current drawing elements and creates a versioned snapshot. 'elements' is the full drawing element array. 'isAutoSave': false = manual (labeled) save; true = auto-save. Test script saves versionId.
Returns the full current drawing state as an array. HTTP fallback for reconnection sync — normally the frontend uses the Socket.IO 'request-sync' event instead.
Lists canvas version snapshots (newest first). Elements are excluded from list — only metadata returned. Use Restore Version to apply a specific snapshot.
Restores the canvas to a historical snapshot. Owner/moderator only. Automatically creates a pre-restore snapshot so you can undo. {{versionId}} is auto-saved by Save Canvas test script.
Returns all notifications for the authenticated user. Test script saves first notification's _id into {{notificationId}}.
Returns the count of unread notifications for the authenticated user. Route: GET /api/notifications/unread/count
Marks a single notification as read. Route: POST /api/notifications/:id/read
Marks all of the authenticated user's notifications as read in bulk.
Permanently deletes a specific notification. Route: DELETE /api/notifications/:id

The following features are handled EXCLUSIVELY via Socket.IO WebSocket and have NO REST equivalents:

• Drawing (strokes, shapes, text, images, eraser)
• Undo / Redo
• Clear Canvas
• Layers
• Cursor position broadcast
• Chat messages
• User presence (joined/left)
• Object locking
• Canvas sync (request-sync event)

To test these, connect a Socket.IO client to ws://localhost:5000 with the JWT token in the auth handshake, then join a room and emit the appropriate events.

Key socket events (roomSocket.js):
- join-room
- drawing-update
- request-sync
- cursor-move
- send-message
- element-lock / element-unlock
- leave-room

item.name: 🧪 E2E Testing Scenarios
item.description: Complete end-to-end test scenarios. Run each sub-folder top-to-bottom using the Postman Collection Runner. Variables are auto-chained between requests via test scripts. All scenarios require the backend running at {{base_url}} (default: http://localhost:5000).

item.name: Scenario 1 — User Registration and Login Flow
item.description:
Tests the full user lifecycle: register → verify email → login → view profile → update profile → change password → delete account.

Expected Result: User successfully registers, logs in, manages profile, and deletes the account.

request.description: Registers a new test user. Response should be 201 with success:true.

exec: pm.test('S1-2: Login returns JWT token', function () {
console.log('✅ S1-2 Login: PASSED — jwt_token saved');

request.description:
Logs in and saves jwt_token for subsequent requests.

NOTE: Email must be verified first (see backend auto-verify logic for emails containing 'testuser').

exec: pm.test('S1-3: Profile returns user data', function () {

request.description: Retrieves the authenticated user's full profile.
Updates bio and display name for the test user.
Changes password. Uses the original registration password as currentPassword.
Deletes the account using the NEW password (changed in S1-5).

item.name: Scenario 2 — Room Creation and Collaboration Flow
item.description:
Tests the full room lifecycle: login → create room → list public rooms → join room → validate access → get participants → invite user → leave room.

Expected Result: Users can create, join, invite participants, and leave collaborative rooms successfully.

request.description: ⚠️ Update email/password to match a verified account in your DB before running.

exec: console.log('✅ S2-2 Create Room: PASSED — roomId: ' + json.room._id + ', roomCode: ' + json.room.roomCode);

request.description: Creates a public test room. Auto-saves roomId and roomCode.

exec: pm.test('S2-3: Public rooms list returned', function () {
console.log('✅ S2-3 Public Rooms: PASSED — ' + json.rooms.length + ' rooms found');

request.description: Lists public rooms. No auth required.

exec: pm.test('S2-4: Room validation returns access info', function () {

request.description: Validates room existence and current user's access level.

exec: console.log('✅ S2-5 Participants: PASSED — ' + json.participants.length + ' participant(s)');

request.description: Gets the participant list for the created room.

exec: // Owners may get a specific message — accept either success or owner error
console.log('✅ S2-6 Leave Room: PASSED — ' + json.message);

request.description: Leaves the room. Note: room owners cannot leave — they must delete the room. This test accepts either outcome.

item.name: Scenario 3 — Canvas Drawing and Persistence Flow
item.description:
Tests canvas save/retrieve/version/restore/export: login → create/join room → save canvas → get state → list versions → restore version → export.

Expected Result: Canvas drawings are saved, retrieved, versioned, restored, and exported successfully.

request.description: ⚠️ Update email/password before running.

exec: console.log('✅ S3-2 Create Room: PASSED — roomId: ' + json.room._id);

request.description: Creates a room for canvas testing.

exec: pm.test('S3-3: Canvas saved with versionId', function () {
console.log('✅ S3-3 Save Canvas: PASSED — versionId: ' + json.versionId);

request.description: Saves a test drawing with a rectangle element. Auto-saves versionId.
Retrieves the current canvas elements array.

exec: console.log('✅ S3-5 List Versions: PASSED — ' + json.versions.length + ' version(s)');

request.description: Lists all canvas version snapshots for the room.
Restores the canvas to the version saved in S3-3. Uses {{versionId}} auto-saved by S3-3.

exec: pm.test('S3-7: Canvas export returns data', function () {

request.description: Exports the canvas as JSON. Use format=png or format=svg for binary/SVG output.

item.name: Scenario 4 — Notification Handling Flow
item.description:
Tests full notification lifecycle: login → get notifications → check unread count → mark as read → delete.

Expected Result: Notifications are retrieved, marked as read, and deleted successfully.

request.description: ⚠️ Update email/password before running.

exec: console.log('✅ S4-2 Notifications: PASSED — notificationId saved: ' + json.notifications[0]._id);
console.log('⚠️ S4-2 Notifications: PASSED but no notifications exist — S4-4 and S4-5 may fail');

request.description: Retrieves all notifications. Auto-saves first notification's _id.

exec: console.log('✅ S4-3 Unread Count: PASSED — count: ' + json.count);

request.description: Returns the number of unread notifications.

exec: pm.test('S4-4: Notification marked as read', function () {
console.log('✅ S4-4 Mark as Read: PASSED');

request.description: Marks the notification from S4-2 as read. Requires {{notificationId}} to be set.
Permanently deletes the notification from S4-2.

item.name: Scenario 5 — Complete System Workflow
item.description:
Full end-to-end system test covering all major subsystems: register → login → create room → save canvas → export canvas → leave room.

Expected Result: The entire system workflow functions correctly without errors.

request.description: Registers a new system test user. Email contains 'testuser' so it auto-verifies.

request.description: Logs in the user registered in S5-1.

exec: console.log('✅ S5-3 Create Room: PASSED — roomId: ' + json.room._id);

request.description: Creates the room for the complete system test.
Saves a canvas with a circle and text element.
Exports the canvas as JSON. Change format to 'png' or 'svg' as needed.

exec: pm.test('S5-6: Room deleted / system test complete', function () {
console.log('🎉 SCENARIO 5 COMPLETE — ALL SYSTEMS OPERATIONAL');

request.description: Deletes the room created in S5-3. Cleans up roomId and roomCode variables.
